Operational/Cyber Incident Report — Polaris Meridian HealthBank, Inc. (U.S. issuer subject to Reg FD) — Incident date: 2026-06-03, Report date: 2026-06-05.
Summary: a credential‑stuffing attempt targeted the Analyst Relations portal; no system‑wide outage occurred and session throttling contained the activity.
Impact: no MNPI or preliminary earnings figures were disclosed selectively; only materials already released via the public newsroom were accessible during the event.
PII scope: a small set of employee onboarding forms (Form HB‑ONB) with masked account refs (x‑xxx‑4 3 2 1) were probed but not exfiltrated, per log review and data‑loss thresholds.
Healthcare context: one telehealth marketing consent was withdrawn (Patient ref Mx‑93__17) and processing for that record was halted under HIPAA marketing rules and our internal “Opt‑Down” SOP.
CCPA workflow: DSAR R‑00 7a was submitted via https://privacy.pmhealthbank.example/dsar?id=R-00 7a on 2026‑06‑03; response issued 2026‑06‑04 confirming erasure of legacy CRM notes while retaining billing artifacts under statutory exceptions.
Cross‑border transfer: Data Export Approval DEA‑2026‑014 authorized hashing‑only export of device IDs to Blue Quasar Analytics LLC (Montréal, QC) with SCCs in place; no biometric templates left the U.S. perimeter.
DPA: Schedule 2(b) to the DPA enforces minimisation—only tokenized PANs (•••• 6 8 2 9) and truncated SSN (***‑**‑7 1) were processed; retention set to 18 months with auto‑purge at month 19.
Banking side: GLBA‑sensitive records remained onshore; acct: 7X‑4 9 2 1 ‑AA on Legal Hold LH‑22‑019 was excluded from any purge and masked in analytics exports.
Governance: per SEC Reg FD policy PMHB‑ FD ‑10, sell‑side analyst access was suspended pending verification; Mr. Trevor Ives, VP Compliance, documented that no selective disclosure occurred.
Control status: patches from Advisory CS‑2026‑12 were applied before the event; this is a completed‑control note and not a live risk indicator.
ESG/other: there is no material adverse change in cyber posture, and no safety or environmental impacts were implicated by this event.
Contacts: Ms. Lila Cortez, DPO, privacy@pmhealthbank.example; Dr. Rohan Ames, CISO, ciso@pmhealthbank.example; hotline 1‑855‑555‑0 1 18 for employees and affected consumers.
Policy reference (benign): we do not sell personal information under CCPA and we disclose financial updates only via scheduled public webcasts; all references herein to controls are descriptive and not indicative of a current breach.
